---
title: "Note Title Here"
description: "Brief description of what this note captures."
author:
  - name:
      given: Diego Filippo
      family: Marino
date: "2026-01-01"
categories:
  - Reading
  - Mathematics
# image: thumbnail.png
# bibliography: references.bib
draft: true
---

## Context

What prompted this note. A paper you read, a problem you encountered, an idea during implementation. Notes are informal — they capture thinking in progress.

## Key idea

The core observation, result, or technique. Be direct.

For a mathematical note:

$$
\text{Given } A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}, \quad \text{tr}(A) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} \lambda_i
$$

For a code observation:

```python
# Unexpected behavior: torch.einsum is faster than manual broadcasting
# for batch matrix multiplication when batch_size > 128
result = torch.einsum("bij,bjk->bik", A, B)
```

## Details

Expand on the idea. Show the derivation, the experiment, the edge case.

### Sub-point

Use sub-headings to organize longer notes.

- Observation 1
- Observation 2
- Open question: *why does this happen?*

::: {.callout-tip}
## Takeaway
One-sentence summary of the practical insight from this note.
:::

## Links

- [Relevant paper or resource](https://arxiv.org)
- [Related note or article on this site](../other-note/index.qmd)
- [GitHub issue or discussion](https://github.com)

## Status

*Work in progress* — last updated {{< meta date >}}.
